message:
fix(fnp-python): RAII AttrGuard restores numpy state on panic (br-franken_numpy-4jlw)

Four tests mutate global numpy attributes (numpy.frompyfunc,
numpy.ma.minimum_fill_value, numpy.linalg.pinv, numpy.linalg.eigvals) to
sentinel 'bomb' lambdas to prove our code paths do NOT delegate back to
numpy. The previous pattern manually restored each attribute after an
assertion closure, but an assertion panic inside the closure would
unwind past the restore and leave numpy permanently poisoned — causing
unrelated parallel tests (count_nonzero_matches_numpy_axis_none,
frompyfunc_live_callable_matches_numpy_reduce_scalar_where_and_direct_out,
etc.) to observe the polluted global state and fail non-deterministically.

Replace the manual save/restore with an AttrGuard RAII helper whose Drop
impl re-acquires the GIL and re-sets the attribute to the captured
original. Restoration now happens on normal return, ? propagation, AND
panic unwind — eliminating the cross-test state leak.

Verified: cargo test -p fnp-python --lib (default parallel threads)
passes 380/380 across two consecutive runs.
